title: Emission Limit Exceedance Response

metadata.title: Emission Limit Exceedance Response
metadata.typical_impact: Regulatory violation, potential enforcement action, fines, community complaints

description: Six-step response procedure when a cement plant exceeds an emission limit detected by CEMS or stack testing.

steps.question: Is the emission exceedance confirmed as real (not a CEMS malfunction)?
steps.action: Verify the exceedance is real. Check CEMS calibration status. Confirm the emission value against the correct limit. If confirmed, immediately notify plant environmental manager and operations. Begin documenting timeline.
check: Verify the exceedance is real (not a CEMS malfunction, calibration drift, or data system error)
Check CEMS calibration status and recent QA/QC results
Confirm the emission value against the correct limit (proper units, O2 correction, averaging period)
If exceedance is confirmed, immediately notify plant environmental manager and operations
Begin documenting timeline: when exceedance started, when detected, actions taken
steps.expected_outcome: Confirmed real exceedance with documentation initiated

steps.question: What is causing the exceedance and can it be mitigated immediately?
steps.action: Review process conditions at time of exceedance. Check for equipment malfunctions. Review alternative fuel feed rate and quality. Reduce kiln feed rate or AF rate if needed. Activate backup emission control equipment if available.
check: Review process conditions at time of exceedance (kiln feed rate, fuel rate, temperatures, pressures)
Check for equipment malfunctions (baghouse, SNCR, scrubber, fan failures)
Review alternative fuel feed rate and quality if AF was in use
Reduce kiln feed rate or AF rate if needed to bring emissions into compliance
Activate backup emission control equipment if available
If exceedance continues despite adjustments, consider controlled shutdown
steps.expected_outcome: Emissions returned to within permit limits, root cause identified

steps.question: Have all required regulatory notifications been submitted within deadlines?
steps.action: Determine notification requirements per Title V permit. Prepare written notification. Submit to permitting authority. Notify local air quality agency if required. Document all notifications made.
check: Determine notification requirements per Title V permit (prompt, 24-hour, 48-hour, or 10-day)
Prepare written notification including: date/time, pollutant, emission level, limit, duration, cause, corrective action
Submit to permitting authority (state air agency and EPA region if required)
Notify local air quality agency if required by permit or local rules
Document all notifications made (to whom, when, method, reference numbers)
steps.expected_outcome: All required regulatory notifications completed within deadlines

steps.question: What is the root cause of the emission exceedance?
steps.action: Conduct formal root cause investigation (5-Why or similar methodology). Review DCS trend data. Interview operators on duty. Check maintenance records. Evaluate preventability. Determine if this is a repeat occurrence.
check: Conduct formal root cause investigation (5-Why or similar methodology)
Review DCS trend data for all related process parameters
Interview operators on duty during the event
Check maintenance records for recent work on emission control equipment
Evaluate whether the exceedance was preventable with existing controls and procedures
Determine if this is a repeat occurrence (check deviation history)

steps.question: Have corrective actions been implemented and verified effective?
steps.action: Develop corrective action plan addressing root cause. Implement immediate fixes. Schedule longer-term corrective actions. Update operating procedures. Retrain operators if needed. Verify effectiveness through follow-up monitoring.
check: Develop corrective action plan addressing root cause (not just symptoms)
Implement immediate fixes (repair equipment, adjust operating parameters)
Schedule longer-term corrective actions with responsible persons and deadlines
Update operating procedures if procedural gaps contributed to the event
Retrain operators if the event involved human error or inadequate response
Verify corrective actions are effective through follow-up monitoring
steps.expected_outcome: Corrective actions implemented and verified effective

steps.question: Is all documentation complete and are preventive measures in place?
steps.action: Complete deviation report for compliance report. Update annual compliance certification. Add event to incident tracking system. Review and update emergency response procedures. Evaluate additional monitoring or redundancy needs. Share lessons learned.
check: Complete deviation report for inclusion in semi-annual compliance report
Update the annual compliance certification to reflect the exceedance
Add event to plant environmental incident tracking system
Review and update emergency response procedures for emission control failures
Evaluate whether additional monitoring or redundancy is needed to prevent recurrence
Share lessons learned with operations and maintenance teams
steps.expected_outcome: Complete documentation and preventive measures in place

key_reminders: Time is critical: notification deadlines are strict and non-negotiable
Document everything from the moment the exceedance is detected
Do not delete or alter any CEMS data (data integrity is legally required)
The emergency affirmative defense (if applicable) requires proving the event was beyond control
Consult with environmental legal counsel for significant or repeat exceedances
Voluntary self-disclosure may mitigate penalties (EPA Audit Policy)
